stem: Evaluate the scalar line integral \( \displaystyle\int_C f\,ds \), where \( f(x,y) = [[a]]x + [[b]]y \) and \( C \) is the segment \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[p]]t,\; [[q]]t) \) for \( 0 \le t \le [[T]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( ds=dt \), forgetting the arc-length speed factor \( |\mathbf{r}'(t)| \), and also drops the factor of \( t \).
This treats \( f(\mathbf{r}(t)) \) as constant over \( [0,[[T]]] \) instead of integrating the linear-in-\(t\) integrand.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is half the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Evaluate the work integral \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( [[a]]y,\; [[b]]x^{2} \right) \) and \( C \) is the straight segment from \( ([[x1]],[[y1]]) \) to \( ([[x2]],[[y2]]) \), traversed once directly.
solution: Parametrize \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[x1]],[[y1]]) + t\big(([[x2]])-([[x1]]),\,([[y2]])-([[y1]])\big) \) for \( 0\le t\le 1 \), so \( d\mathbf{r} = \big(([[x2]])-([[x1]]),\,([[y2]])-([[y1]])\big)dt \). Substituting into \( \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \) and integrating from \( t=0 \) to \( t=1 \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( \mathbf{F} \) only at the displacement vector itself, skipping the actual parametrized integration.
This evaluates \( \mathbf{F} \) only at the endpoint \( B \) and multiplies by the displacement, instead of integrating along the path.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This double counts the work integral.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C [[a]]xy\,dx + [[b]]y\,dy \), where \( C \) is the portion of the curve \( y = x^{2} \) from \( x=[[x1]] \) to \( x=[[x2]] \).
solution: On \( C \), substitute \( y=x^{2} \) and \( dy=2x\,dx \), so the integral becomes a single-variable integral in \( x \): \( \int_{[[x1]]}^{[[x2]]} \big[ ([[a]])x(x^{2}) + ([[b]])(x^{2})(2x) \big]\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the \( [[b]]y\,dy \) term entirely, integrating only the \( dx \)-piece.
This drops the \( [[a]]xy\,dx \) term entirely, integrating only the \( dy \)-piece.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is half the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( [[a]]z,\; [[b]]x,\; [[c]]y \right) \) and let \( C \) be one full turn of the helix \( \mathbf{r}(t) = ([[R]]\cos t,\; [[R]]\sin t,\; [[k]]t) \) for \( 0 \le t \le 2\pi \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \).
solution: With \( \mathbf{r}'(t) = (-[[R]]\sin t,\,[[R]]\cos t,\,[[k]]) \), \( \mathbf{F}(\mathbf{r}(t))\cdot\mathbf{r}'(t) = -([[a]])([[k]])[[R]]\,t\sin t + ([[b]])[[R]]^{2}\cos^{2}t + ([[c]])[[R]]\cdot[[k]]\sin t \). Over \( 0\le t\le 2\pi \) the last term vanishes (a full period of \( \sin t \)), \( \int_0^{2\pi} t\sin t\,dt = -2\pi \), and \( \int_0^{2\pi}\cos^{2}t\,dt = \pi \), giving \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = 2\pi([[a]])([[k]])([[R]]) + \pi([[b]])([[R]])^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This flips the sign of the \( ([[a]])([[k]]) \)-term relative to the \( ([[b]])([[R]]) \)-term.
This uses \( \int_0^{2\pi}\cos^2 t\,dt = 2\pi \) (the period length) instead of the correct value \( \pi \).
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is half the correct value.
This adds a spurious \( \pi \) to the correct value.
This subtracts a spurious \( \pi \) from the correct value.
